wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Narrative Operating System project overview. Explain its purpose as an AI-powered CLI tool for automated story generation, its core architecture as a monorepo with CLI application and engine packages, and relationship to other components. Document the implementation philosophy, key features including interactive story creation, AI agent system, and canonical memory management. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase such as 'StoryBible', 'CanonStore', and 'GenerationPipeline'. Provide practical examples demonstrating the complete workflow from initialization to chapter generation. Document the monorepo structure, package relationships, and development approach.

wiki_catalogs.name: Story Bible Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Story Bible Management system. Document the StoryBible data structure including metadata fields (title, theme, genre, setting, tone), character profile management with personality traits and goals, and plot thread development with tension mechanics. Explain the createStoryBible function for initial story setup, addCharacter for character development workflows, and addPlotThread for narrative thread management. Include practical examples of story creation processes, character arc development, and plot thread integration. Document the immutable update pattern used throughout the system and ID generation strategies. Address story metadata validation, character relationship modeling, and plot thread lifecycle management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Writer Agent
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Writer Agent responsible for chapter creation and narrative prose generation. Detail the ChapterWriter class implementation including prompt construction, context processing, and LLM integration patterns. Document the writing workflow from story bible processing to chapter completion, including character integration, tone maintenance, and goal-oriented writing. Explain the chapter continuation mechanism, title extraction logic, and word count management. Include practical examples of prompt template usage, parameter configuration, and output formatting. Address writing guidelines enforcement, narrative consistency maintenance, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core engine components that form the foundation of the Narrative Operating System. Document the main export structure including LLM client, agents, pipeline, story management, and memory systems. Explain the modular architecture design and how components are organized for maximum reusability. Detail the type system including LLMProviderConfig, GenerateChapterResult, and other core interfaces. Include examples of how these components work together and their role in the overall system architecture. Address component initialization patterns and dependency injection approaches used throughout the engine.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Narrative Operating System CLI tool. Include detailed installation instructions using PNPM workspace setup, initial configuration steps for LLM providers (OpenAI, DeepSeek), and step-by-step first story creation process. Document the complete workflow from `nos config` through `nos init` to `nos generate`. Provide practical examples of story initialization parameters, character creation, and plot development. Include troubleshooting common setup issues, environment requirements, and verification steps. Address prerequisite knowledge including TypeScript basics and command-line familiarity. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for quick onboarding.

wiki_catalogs.name: Runtime Story State
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Runtime Story State management system. Detail the StoryState data model including chapter progression tracking, word count statistics, completion metrics, and generation history. Document state mutation patterns, immutable state updates, and state validation mechanisms. Explain the relationship between StoryBible and StoryState, including how story metadata translates to runtime progress tracking. Include examples of state transitions during chapter generation, completion calculations, and progress monitoring. Document state persistence strategies, state restoration after interruptions, and state validation for corrupted data. Address performance considerations for large stories and state cleanup mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Completeness Agent
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Completeness Agent that validates narrative quality and ensures chapter completeness. Document the validation logic including plot thread adherence, character consistency checks, and structural completeness assessment. Explain the prompt engineering approach used for quality evaluation, scoring mechanisms, and decision-making algorithms. Include examples of validation criteria, failure detection patterns, and remediation strategies. Address the agent's role in the generation pipeline, feedback loops, and integration with other validation systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Integration Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the LLM integration layer that abstracts Large Language Model providers. Document the LLMClient architecture, provider abstraction patterns, and configuration management for different LLM providers including OpenAI and DeepSeek. Explain the factory pattern implementation for dynamic provider instantiation based on configuration. Include concrete examples of provider setup, API key management, and fallback mechanisms. Document the client interface, method signatures, and error handling strategies. Address performance considerations, rate limiting, and cost optimization techniques for LLM usage.

wiki_catalogs.name: CLI Command Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive CLI command reference documentation for all Narrative Operating System commands. Document command syntax, parameters, flags, and usage patterns for each command including `nos config`, `nos init`, `nos generate`, `nos status`, and `nos continue`. Explain command relationships, input/output formats, and configuration options. Include practical examples demonstrating common workflows, batch operations, and automation scenarios. Document exit codes, error handling, and troubleshooting steps for each command. Address advanced usage patterns, scripting integration, and performance considerations. Provide both basic usage examples for beginners and power-user techniques for experienced developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Local Storage & Persistence
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Local Storage and Persistence system. Document the filesystem organization for story data, including directory structure, file naming conventions, and data serialization formats. Explain the ConfigStore interface for reading and writing story configurations, including story metadata, state snapshots, and generated content. Detail backup and recovery mechanisms, data migration strategies between versions, and conflict resolution for concurrent access. Include examples of story loading workflows, state restoration after application restart, and manual backup procedures. Document storage limits, cleanup policies, and performance optimization for large story datasets. Address cross-platform compatibility and filesystem permissions handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Summarizer Agent
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Summarizer Agent responsible for chapter synthesis and narrative condensation. Detail the summarization algorithm, key point extraction techniques, and narrative essence preservation methods. Document the prompt construction for different summarization styles, length optimization strategies, and coherence maintenance approaches. Include examples of summarization outputs, quality metrics, and integration with the generation pipeline. Address summarization context management, temporal coherence preservation, and performance considerations for large chapters.

wiki_catalogs.name: Memory Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the canonical memory management system that ensures narrative consistency. Document the CanonStore architecture, fact extraction from story bibles, and memory validation processes. Explain the data structures for CanonFact and CanonStore, including storage mechanisms and retrieval patterns. Detail the memory extraction algorithms, fact categorization, and integration with the generation pipeline. Include examples of memory operations, canonical fact validation, and prompt formatting. Address memory persistence, backup strategies, and performance optimization for large story datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Engine Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Narrative Operating System engine package. Describe the high-level design of the AI-powered story generation system, architectural patterns including factory pattern for LLM providers and pipeline pattern for generation workflow. Document component interactions between agents, memory systems, and LLM integration layers. Explain technical decisions around agent-based architecture, canonical memory management, and modular design. Include system boundaries, data flows from StoryBible through GenerationPipeline to final chapters. Address cross-cutting concerns like configuration management, error handling, and extensibility. Document technology stack including TypeScript, OpenAI SDK integration, and monorepo orchestration with Turborepo.

wiki_catalogs.name: Canon Validator Agent
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Canon Validator Agent that maintains narrative consistency and prevents contradictions. Document the validation algorithms for fact checking, character continuity, and plot thread consistency. Explain the integration with the canonical memory store, contradiction detection mechanisms, and resolution strategies. Include examples of validation scenarios, false positive handling, and edge case management. Address the agent's role in maintaining story integrity, handling ambiguous references, and coordinating with other validation systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Generation Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the chapter generation pipeline that orchestrates the AI-powered story creation process. Document the generateChapter function workflow, step-by-step processing stages, and integration with various agents. Explain the pipeline architecture including input validation, context preparation, agent coordination, and output synthesis. Detail the GenerateChapterOptions interface, result structures, and error handling mechanisms. Include examples of pipeline execution, parameter configuration, and performance monitoring. Address pipeline extensibility, custom processing steps, and debugging strategies for generation failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Story Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Story Management System within the Narrative Operating System. Detail the StoryBible structure for story metadata and character profiles, StoryState management for runtime progression tracking, and local filesystem storage mechanisms. Document the complete lifecycle from story initialization through chapter generation to completion. Explain data models, serialization formats, and persistence strategies. Include practical examples of story creation workflows, character development processes, and plot thread management. Address storage organization, backup strategies, and migration between versions. Document integration with CLI commands and automated generation pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Agent System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the AI Agent System powering narrative generation. Explain the agent architecture including Writer Agent for chapter creation, Completeness Agent for quality validation, Summarizer Agent for chapter synthesis, and CanonValidator Agent for narrative consistency. Document agent responsibilities, communication patterns, and coordination mechanisms. Detail the prompt engineering approach, LLM integration patterns, and parameter configuration for each agent. Include practical examples of agent interactions, decision-making processes, and error handling. Address agent customization, performance optimization, and debugging techniques. Document the relationship between agents and the overall generation pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Memory Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Memory Management System including Canonical Fact Storage and Memory Validation. Detail the CanonStore implementation for persistent canonical fact management, memory extraction from story bibles, and integration with generation pipeline. Document memory validation algorithms, fact consistency checking, and conflict resolution mechanisms. Explain memory lifecycle from extraction through validation to integration. Include practical examples of memory usage in chapter generation, canonical fact prioritization, and memory growth strategies. Address memory optimization, storage efficiency, and performance considerations. Document the relationship between memory system and AI agents for maintaining narrative coherence.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Integration and Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for LLM Integration and Configuration within the Narrative Operating System. Document the provider abstraction layer supporting OpenAI and DeepSeek, configuration management for API keys and model parameters, and prompt engineering strategies. Explain the client architecture, connection pooling, rate limiting, and error handling mechanisms. Include practical examples of provider switching, model selection criteria, and cost optimization strategies. Address configuration validation, environment variable management, and security considerations. Document the relationship between LLM configuration and agent performance, including parameter tuning and prompt optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development and Contributing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development and contributing documentation for the Narrative Operating System project. Document the monorepo development environment setup using PNPM workspaces and Turborepo orchestration, build system configuration, and development workflow. Explain testing strategies including unit tests for the engine package, integration testing approaches, and continuous integration setup. Include contribution guidelines, code style standards, and pull request procedures. Document debugging techniques, performance profiling, and development tools. Address release processes, version management, and dependency updates. Provide guidance for extending the system with custom agents, memory strategies, and LLM providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Narrative Operating System public interfaces. Document TypeScript type definitions, exported interfaces, and class specifications from the engine package. Include detailed parameter descriptions, return value specifications, and usage examples for all public APIs. Document the StoryBible interface, StoryState management, and agent interfaces. Explain type safety mechanisms, generic constraints, and interface inheritance patterns. Include practical examples demonstrating API usage in custom integrations, extension development, and third-party applications. Address API versioning, backward compatibility considerations, and migration guides for breaking changes.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Narrative OS: AI-Powered Story Generation Platform

## Project Introduction

**Narrative OS** is an innovative AI-powered story generation platform designed to create immersive, coherent narratives through a sophisticated pipeline of intelligent agents. The project serves as both a command-line interface tool for individual writers and a foundation for scalable web services that can generate and manage complex literary works.

### Purpose Statement
The platform aims to democratize AI-assisted storytelling by providing a robust, extensible framework that maintains narrative consistency while leveraging large language models to generate compelling prose.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Autonomous Story Generation**: Create complete novels with consistent character development and plot progression
- **Narrative Consistency**: Implement memory systems that prevent factual contradictions across chapters
- **Scalable Architecture**: Support both local CLI usage and cloud-based service deployments
- **Extensible Framework**: Provide modular components that can be enhanced with additional narrative capabilities

### Target Audience
- Independent authors seeking AI assistance in their creative process
- Game developers requiring dynamic narrative generation
- Educational institutions teaching creative writing with AI tools
- Companies building AI-powered storytelling applications

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a monorepo architecture using Turborepo for build orchestration, organized into distinct packages and applications:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Narrative OS Monorepo] --> B[CLI Application]
    A --> C[Engine Package]
    A --> D[Web API Server]
    A --> E[Worker Service]
    
    B --> F[Command Interface]
    B --> G[Story Management]
    
    C --> H[LLM Client]
    C --> I[Agent System]
    C --> J[Memory Management]
    C --> K[Pipeline Engine]
    
    I --> L[Chapter Writer]
    I --> M[Completeness Checker]
    I --> N[Summarizer]
    I --> O[Canon Validator]
    
    J --> P[Canon Store]
    J --> Q[Vector Memory]
    
    K --> R[Generation Pipeline]
    K --> S[Validation Loop]
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture consists of several key layers:

**Application Layer**
- CLI application for local story creation and management
- Web API server for scalable story generation services
- Worker service for asynchronous background processing

**Core Engine Layer**
- LLM abstraction layer supporting multiple providers
- Agent-based narrative generation system
- Memory management with canonical fact preservation
- Pipeline orchestration for chapter generation

**Data Layer**
- Local filesystem storage for story persistence
- Configurable memory stores for narrative facts
- Vector databases for semantic memory retrieval

### Design Patterns

The system employs several key design patterns:

- **Factory Pattern**: LLM provider instantiation based on configuration
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable memory and validation strategies
- **Pipeline Pattern**: Sequential processing of narrative generation
- **Observer Pattern**: State change notifications throughout the system

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**CLI Application**
- Primary entry: `apps/cli/src/index.ts`
- Command structure: `apps/cli/src/commands/`
- Configuration management: `apps/cli/src/config/store.ts`

**Engine Core**
- Package entry: `packages/engine/src/index.ts`
- Type definitions: `packages/engine/src/types/index.ts`
- LLM abstraction: `packages/engine/src/llm/client.ts`

### Core Modules

**Story Management System**
- Story creation and persistence: `packages/engine/src/story/bible.ts`
- Dynamic story state tracking: `packages/engine/src/story/state.ts`
- Local storage implementation: `apps/cli/src/config/store.ts`

**Agent System**
- Chapter generation: `packages/engine/src/agents/writer.ts`
- Quality validation: `packages/engine/src/agents/completeness.ts`
- Summary generation: `packages/engine/src/agents/summarizer.ts`
- Canon validation: `packages/engine/src/agents/canonValidator.ts`

**Memory Management**
- Canonical fact storage: `packages/engine/src/memory/canonStore.ts`
- Generation pipeline: `packages/engine/src/pipeline/generateChapter.ts`

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**
- Provider selection: `LLM_PROVIDER`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`
- Model settings: `LLM_MODEL`, `LLM_TEMPERATURE`, `LLM_MAX_TOKENS`
- Storage locations: `~/.narrative-os/` for story data

**Build System**
- Task orchestration: `turbo.json` with caching and dependency management
- Workspace definition: `pnpm-workspace.yaml` for monorepo structure
- Package management: `package.json` with PNPM workspace support

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies**
- `openai`: Official OpenAI SDK for GPT integration
- `commander`: CLI argument parsing and command framework
- `@inquirer/prompts`: Interactive user interface components
- `zod`: Runtime type validation for configuration data

**Development Dependencies**
- `turbo`: Build system and task orchestration
- `typescript`: Type safety and modern JavaScript features
- `@types/node`: Node.js type definitions

### Integration Points

**LLM Provider Abstraction**
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant LLMClient
    participant Provider
    participant LLM
    
    Client->>+LLMClient: complete(prompt)
    LLMClient->>+Provider: complete(prompt)
    Provider->>+LLM: chat.completions.create()
    LLM-->>-Provider: response
    Provider-->>-LLMClient: content
    LLMClient-->>-Client: processed response
```

**Story Generation Pipeline**
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Story Context] --> B[Chapter Writer]
    B --> C[Quality Check]
    C --> D{Complete?}
    D -->|No| E[Continue Writing]
    D -->|Yes| F[Canon Validation]
    E --> C
    F --> G[Summary Generation]
    G --> H[Chapter Storage]
    H --> I[Story State Update]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Story Creation and Management**
- Interactive story initialization with configurable parameters
- Automatic character and plot thread generation
- Persistent story state management across sessions

**Advanced Narrative Generation**
- Multi-agent writing system with quality control
- Canonical fact preservation preventing narrative inconsistencies
- Semantic memory integration for contextual coherence

**Flexible Deployment Options**
- Standalone CLI for individual use
- Scalable API server for team collaboration
- Background worker system for high-volume generation

### Implementation Highlights

**Modular Agent Architecture**
- `packages/engine/src/agents/`: Specialized agents for different narrative tasks
- `packages/engine/src/memory/`: Sophisticated memory management systems
- `packages/engine/src/pipeline/`: Coordinated workflow orchestration

**Configuration Management**
- `apps/cli/src/commands/config.ts`: Interactive LLM provider setup
- Environment variable-based configuration system
- Cross-platform compatibility with Windows, macOS, and Linux

**Storage and Persistence**
- Hierarchical filesystem organization under `~/.narrative-os/`
- JSON-based data serialization for human readability
- Automatic backup and recovery mechanisms

### Feature Architecture

**State Management Flow**
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Creating
    Creating --> Generating: Initialize Story
    Generating --> Validating: Chapter Complete
    Validating --> Storing: Validation Pass
    Storing --> Generating: Next Chapter
    Generating --> [*]: Story Complete
    Validating --> Generating: Fix Issues
```

**Memory Integration Process**
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Story Bible] --> B[Canon Extraction]
    B --> C[Canon Store]
    C --> D[Writer Prompt Injection]
    D --> E[Chapter Generation]
    E --> F[Canon Validation]
    F --> G[Violation Reporting]
    G --> H[Memory Updates]
    H --> C
```

**Development Workflow**
- Progressive feature implementation following the 10-phase plan
- Integration testing with real LLM providers
- Continuous improvement through iterative development cycles

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [pnpm-workspace.yaml](pnpm-workspace.yaml)
- [turbo.json](turbo.json)
- [apps/cli/package.json](apps/cli/package.json)
- [packages/engine/package.json](packages/engine/package.json)
- [apps/cli/src/index.ts](apps/cli/src/index.ts)
- [packages/engine/src/index.ts](packages/engine/src/index.ts)
- [packages/engine/src/types/index.ts](packages/engine/src/types/index.ts)
- [packages/engine/src/llm/client.ts](packages/engine/src/llm/client.ts)
- [packages/engine/src/agents/writer.ts](packages/engine/src/agents/writer.ts)
- [packages/engine/src/pipeline/generateChapter.ts](packages/engine/src/pipeline/generateChapter.ts)
- [implementation-plan.md](implementation-plan.md)
- [PROGRESS.md](PROGRESS.md)
- [apps/cli/src/commands/init.ts](apps/cli/src/commands/init.ts)
- [apps/cli/src/config/store.ts](apps/cli/src/config/store.ts)

</blog>
